name: Credit Saint by Liberty Tax
description_short: Credit Saint is a credit repair company that disputes inaccurate negative items on credit reports through challenges to creditors and bureaus. They've served 200k+ customers since 2007 with a 4.8 Google rating.
description_long:
Credit Saint is a credit repair service founded in 2007 and operated by Liberty Tax. The company positions itself as a leader in the credit repair industry, claiming the #1 ranking on multiple financial sites and maintaining a 4.8-star rating across 14,000+ Google reviews. They advertise 19 years of experience and have served over 200,000 customers. The company's brand emphasizes human credit specialists and personalized service rather than automated processes.

Credit Saint's core service is disputing inaccurate or unfair negative items on credit reports. They work by examining client credit reports, identifying problematic items, and then sending formal disputes to creditors and all three credit bureaus (Equifax, Experian, TransUnion). The types of negative items they target include collections accounts, late payments, foreclosures, charge-offs, bankruptcies, judgments, repossessions, and liens. Their process involves three stages: initial setup using a secure online portal, professional review by a credit specialist, and ongoing optimization with advice on maintaining positive credit history.

Credit Saint distinguishes itself through several offerings: a 90-day money-back guarantee, three tiered pricing plans (Credit Polish at $79.99/month, Credit Remodel at $109.99/month, and Clean Slate at $139.99/month), all with first-time work fees ranging from $99-$195, and emphasis on human specialist consultation rather than automation. They promote a secure online portal for document sharing and claim to obtain free credit reports without impacting credit scores. Customer testimonials on their site highlight staff by name (Marsha, Nicole, Vicki) and emphasize professionalism and knowledgeable service.

However, important caveats apply: Credit Saint's website includes a disclaimer that credit service companies cannot guarantee results or specific credit score increases, which is legally required language. The website does not disclose specific dispute success rates, average timelines for results, or details about how their process differs substantively from disputing on one's own. Pricing is subscription-based with ongoing monthly fees, meaning total costs scale with program length. The company's claims of being "ranked #1" are not independently verified on the website itself.
address: 962 W Manchester Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90044

company_info.headquarters: 962 W Manchester Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90044

pros: 4.8-star rating with 14,000+ Google reviews and 11,000+ five-star reviews across multiple platforms (Google, ConsumerAffairs, Money.com)
Three tiered service plans ($79.99-$139.99/month) allowing consumers to choose complexity level matching their needs
90-day money-back guarantee provides financial protection if dissatisfied with results
Human credit specialists review reports and send disputes rather than fully automated system
Secure online portal for document sharing and credit report access without hard inquiry impact
Targets eight specific negative item types (collections, late payments, foreclosure, charge-offs, bankruptcy, judgments, repossessions, liens)
19 years of operating history since 2007 demonstrates longevity in the industry
cons: Subscription-based monthly fees ($79.99-$139.99) mean total cost scales with program duration; no flat-fee option disclosed
Website includes mandatory disclaimer that credit repair companies cannot guarantee results or credit score increases, limiting realistic expectations
No specific dispute success rates, average resolution timelines, or performance metrics publicly disclosed
First-time work fees ($99-$195) add to initial costs before monthly service charges begin
Limited transparency about how their dispute process differs from consumers disputing independently for free
best_for: Consumers with multiple negative items (collections, late payments, charge-offs) who prefer professional representation and don't want to self-dispute
Individuals seeking structured guidance through credit repair with tiered service levels matching complexity of their credit issues
People prioritizing human specialist consultation over fully automated credit repair tools
Consumers who value money-back guarantees and established brand reputation with significant customer reviews
diagnosis: Credit Saint is best suited for consumers with multiple negative credit items who prefer paying for professional dispute representation and human specialist guidance rather than self-disputing or using fully automated tools. The critical caveat is that like all credit repair companies, they cannot guarantee specific results or credit score increases, and their core service (disputing inaccurate items) is something consumers can do independently for free—the value proposition centers on convenience and expertise rather than access to unique dispute mechanisms.
services: Credit report review and analysis by human credit specialists
Formal dispute filing to all three credit bureaus (Equifax, Experian, TransUnion)
Dispute submission to creditors on inaccurate negative items
Ongoing credit optimization advice and positive credit history guidance
